member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.ApplyEvent
member.summary: Single event on the apply stream. The stream begins with , interleaves per plan node, and ends with either , or .

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.ConversationTurn
member.summary: One round-trip in the operator/grounding dialogue. Rendered as a card in the NL pane. The discriminator determines which body the UI shows (mutation diff, clarification picker, or error chip).

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.IntentOutcome
member.summary: Result of submitting an operator intent to the grounding service. Callers branch on ; only one of , or will be populated.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.PlanResult
member.summary: Output of a plan call: the topologically-ordered DAG the server would execute plus the estimated cost of each node.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.SpecDocument
member.summary: Canonical in-memory projection of a spec draft. Five named sections mirror the S1 walking-skeleton resource set (sources, scope, compute, map, output). JSON is the authoritative form; text projections are derived.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.SpecGrammar
member.summary: Projection of the section/operator grammar the server publishes. S1 ships an embedded copy in Resources/spec-grammar.v1.json; once the real server grammar endpoint lands, the deserializer here is unchanged.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.SpecWorkspaceJsonContext
member.summary: Source-generated serializer context for all spec-workspace DTOs. Required because the admin project is AOT/trim friendly; reflection-based JsonSerializer calls would break WebAssembly publishing.

member.name: P:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.SpecWorkspaceJsonContext.Boolean
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.SpecWorkspaceJsonContext.Default
member.summary: The default associated with a default instance.

member.name: P:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.SpecWorkspaceJsonContext.GeneratedSerializerOptions
member.summary: The source-generated options associated with this context.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.ValidationDiagnostic
member.summary: Single diagnostic surfaced next to a section or identifier in the DSL pane. Red = unknown identifier / type mismatch / missing required / unknown op. Yellow = CRS unit mismatch / mutable-source-no-pin / estimated-oversize / non-deterministic op.

member.name: M:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.ValidationDiagnostic.#ctor(Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.SpecSectionId,Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.ValidationSeverity,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Single diagnostic surfaced next to a section or identifier in the DSL pane. Red = unknown identifier / type mismatch / missing required / unknown op. Yellow = CRS unit mismatch / mutable-source-no-pin / estimated-oversize / non-deterministic op.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Models.SpecWorkspace.WorkspaceSnapshot
member.summary: What gets serialized to localStorage for "refresh preserves state". Captures the current spec draft, conversation, layout widths, and principal so different operators sharing a browser get isolated drafts.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.CatalogCache
member.summary: Thread-safe LRU cache sitting in front of . RBAC principal keyed so switching operators wipes stale candidates. Sized for the S1 AC ("IntelliSense completion latency under 100 ms for cached grammar + catalog").

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.DevAuthenticationStateProvider
member.summary: Dev / S1 no-op authentication state provider. The admin auth scaffold is still a TODO in Program.cs; this stub keeps gates additive-only so wiring a real provider later is a drop-in replacement.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.IBrowserStorageService
member.summary: Typed wrapper around browser localStorage. Separated from direct IJSRuntime usage so tests can drop in an in-memory fake.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.ISpecWorkspaceClient
member.summary: Seam between the admin UI and the grounding / planning / execution back-end. S1 ships with ; a gRPC-backed implementation lands in a follow-on admin ticket once the sibling repos publish their surfaces.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.ISpecWorkspaceTelemetry
member.summary: Telemetry adapter for every observable transition in the spec workspace. Kept behind an interface so a shared admin telemetry sink can replace the default ILogger-backed implementation without touching the state store.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.SpecWorkspaceState
member.summary: Scoped observable store that backs the three panes. All mutations funnel through explicit methods so persistence, telemetry, and preview updates stay single-origin.

member.name: T:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.StubSpecWorkspaceClient
member.summary: Deterministic in-memory grounding + planning + execution simulator. S1 uses this to drive every AC through the admin UI so the walking skeleton is demonstrable before the real server/SDK surfaces land. A deliberately small set of regex rules covers the scripted demo flow: pick a dataset, pick an aggregate op, plan, apply, cancel.

member.name: M:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.StubSpecWorkspaceClient.AtRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
@([a-zA-Z_][\\w]*)
Options:
RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match '@'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [A-Z_a-z].
○ Match a word character atomically any number of times.

member.name: M:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.StubSpecWorkspaceClient.AggRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
\\b(aggregate|count|sum|avg|group)\\b
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match if at a word boundary.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match with 5 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Cc].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Mm].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Vv].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Pp].
○ Match if at a word boundary.

member.name: M:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.StubSpecWorkspaceClient.FilterRgx
member.remarks:
Pattern:
\\b(filter|where)\\b
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match if at a word boundary.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ff].
○ Match a character in the set [Ii].
○ Match a character in the set [Ll].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ww].
○ Match a character in the set [Hh].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match if at a word boundary.

member.name: M:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.StubSpecWorkspaceClient.MapRgx
member.remarks:
Pattern:
\\b(map|render|show|plot)\\b
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match if at a word boundary.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match with 4 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Mm].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Pp].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss].
○ Match a character in the set [Hh].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Ww].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Pp].
○ Match a character in the set [Ll].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match if at a word boundary.

member.name: M:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.StubSpecWorkspaceClient.BufRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
buffer\\s+(?:@[\\w]+\\s+)?(?:by\\s+)?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?)\\s*(m|km|mi)?
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match a character in the set [Bb].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Ff] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a whitespace character greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match '@'.
○ Match a word character atomically at least once.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match a character in the set [Bb].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match a whitespace character atomically at least once.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match '.'.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least once.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily any number of times.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match with 3 alternative expressions, atomically.
○ Match a character in the set [Mm].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Kk\u212A].
○ Match a character in the set [Mm].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Mm].
○ Match a character in the set [Ii].

member.name: M:Honua.Admin.Services.SpecWorkspace.StubSpecWorkspaceClient.OpRgx
member.remarks:
Pattern:
\\b(compute|do something|analyse|analyze)\\b
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match if at a word boundary.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match with 3 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Cc].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Mm].
○ Match a character in the set [Pp].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match ' '.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Mm].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Hh].
○ Match a character in the set [Ii].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Ll].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Zz].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match if at a word boundary.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0
member.summary: Custom -derived type for the AtRegex method.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0.Instance
member.summary: Cached, thread-safe singleton instance.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes the instance.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0.RunnerFactory
member.summary: Provides a factory for creating instances to be used by methods on .

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0.RunnerFactory.CreateInstance
member.summary: Creates an instance of a used by methods on .

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner
member.summary: Provides the runner that contains the custom logic implementing the specified regular expression.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.Scan(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Scan the starting from base.runtextstart for the next match.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryFindNextPossibleStartingPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Search starting from base.runtextpos for the next location a match could possibly start.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if a possible match was found; false if no more matches are possible.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.AtRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryMatchAtCurrentPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Determine whether at base.runtextpos is a match for the regular expression.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if the regular expression matches at the current position; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.FilterRgx_2.RunnerFactory.Runner.Scan(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Scan the starting from base.runtextstart for the next match.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.FilterRgx_2.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryFindNextPossibleStartingPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Search starting from base.runtextpos for the next location a match could possibly start.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if a possible match was found; false if no more matches are possible.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.FilterRgx_2.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryMatchAtCurrentPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Determine whether at base.runtextpos is a match for the regular expression.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if the regular expression matches at the current position; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities
member.summary: Helper methods used by generated -derived implementations.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_defaultTimeout
member.summary: Default timeout value set in , or if none was set.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_hasTimeout
member.summary: Whether is non-infinite.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.IsBoundaryWordChar(System.Char)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified index is a boundary word character.
member.remarks: This is the same as \w plus U+200C ZERO WIDTH NON-JOINER and U+200D ZERO WIDTH JOINER.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.IsPostWordCharBoundary(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified index is a boundary.
member.remarks: This variant is only employed when the previous character has already been validated as a word character.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.IsPreWordCharBoundary(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified index is a boundary.
member.remarks: This variant is only employed when the subsequent character will separately be validated as a word character.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.IsWordChar(System.Char)
member.summary: Determines whether the character is part of the [\w] set.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.StackPop(System.Int32[],System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Pops 2 values from the backtracking stack.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.StackPush(System.Int32[]@,System.Int32@,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Pushes 2 values onto the backtracking stack.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.StackPush(System.Int32[]@,System.Int32@,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Pushes 3 values onto the backtracking stack.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.WordCategoriesMask
member.summary: Provides a mask of Unicode categories that combine to form [\w].

member.name: P:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.WordCharBitmap
member.summary: Gets a bitmap for whether each character 0 through 127 is in [\w]

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_indexOfAnyStrings_OrdinalIgnoreCase_0026FEB6E3481DF71F68B3A8EE8A685AFE67857809FC8AA13AC3B8F0F7A1C3B2
member.summary: Supports searching for the specified strings.

member.summary: Supports searching for the specified strings.

member.summary: Supports searching for the specified strings.

member.summary: Supports searching for the specified strings.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_indexOfString_buffer_OrdinalIgnoreCase
member.summary: Supports searching for the string "buffer".
